channel.title: Comments for page "Avian Anthology II"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Avian Anthology II"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’ve just now gotten around to reading all of the material there is to this, and this has got to be one of my favorite tales. Very well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Give us the good news first" replied Kesh, who had also woken up.

This woke up Kesh, who hadn't woken up yet.

These two sentences contradict each other.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Spoilers
- not_a_seagull is just two pigeons in a trench coat

To quote Avian Anthology I:

YHWH encompassed what seemed like the entirety of the mind-filled expanse. If Thoth executed his plans at the wrong time, YHWH would strike him down instantly and bar him from prosperity until the end of time. Luckily, YHWH would soon find himself busy with something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If this contest has taught us anything, it's that what happens in Vegas sure as hell doesn't stay there.

Great conclusion to a very enjoyable series, and wonderful work, all of you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really liked this entry, although i'm still a bit confused about how 3362 fits in all of this, especially that last line about O5-10, which was one of my favourite parts of the entire series.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A shark god ate a bird god, and then they killed it with a giant rocket.

I may not be the biggest fan of this canon, but that ending was metal as fuck. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love that crisis was averted in a typical Team Bird-esque manner, and it was still averted while taking a heavy loss. Nothing was fixed easily, but at least the world got saved. It was a thrilling conclusion, though I have my misgivings. The final confrontation ultimately feels like it was wrapped up way too neatly, and for all of Thoth's build-up, he ultimately didn't feel as threatening as earlier entries made him seem. I still can't help but love it though. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has definitely been my favourite entry in the contest. I mean, I have a significant amount of backlog left for all the things that were posted over the last two days, but I'd be surprised if anything surpassed this one for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wonderful. I would love to see more stories in a post Bird Meme world, but this was a fantastic ride from start to finish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I recently read World War Z, and there was something in it that really captivated me, which was really the idea that humanity got pushed to the brink but was able to still win at the end. The idea of a Doomsday happening but then getting (mostly) fixed was something I really liked about it.

I once saw somebody call the idea of the Foundation saving the day "cliche", which struck me as really bizarre, because in almost every doomsday scenario we see on this site, the Foundation loses badly. There really aren't that many examples of what I described above happening.

Looking at the other doomsday entries, pretty much none (that are finished atm) of them have the "Foundation is pushed to the brink but fights back and wins at the end." End of Death comes close, but it's more about humanity adapting to the apocalypse and not beating the apocalypse.

This scratches that itch, and I love it for it. Seeing humanity manage to pull out a victory at the end is very refreshing.

item.title: Bird Post
item.content:encoded:
Second1 part of the finale, and a collaboration with the rest of Team Bird, aka FloppyPhoenix, not_a_seagull and Quantum Physician. I listened to this song on loop while writing my part of this, so I consider it the official theme of this tale.

Thanks to The Great Hippo and Sir Baubius for critique.

Team Bird Best Team

Footnotes
1. Technically third, but who's counting?
